children.name: Ramphastos
children.description: The large toucans — the iconic toucans with enormous, colourful bills that can reach half the length of the body. The bill is surprisingly light, made of keratin sheathing a honeycomb of air-filled bone. It is used for reaching fruit on thin branches, display, and thermoregulation. Found across Central and South America.

children.name: Ramphastos toco
children.description: The largest and most famous toucan, reaching 63 cm. Black body, white throat, and an enormous orange-yellow bill with a black tip and blue base. Found in the Amazon Basin, Cerrado, and Pantanal of Brazil, Bolivia, Paraguay, and Argentina. The iconic 'toucan' of popular culture.

children.name: Ramphastos sulfuratus
children.description: The multi-coloured 'rainbow-billed' toucan of Central America. Black body, yellow throat, and a bill showcasing all colours of the rainbow — green, orange, red, and blue. Found from southern Mexico to Colombia and Venezuela.

children.name: Ramphastos vitellinus
children.description: Found in the Amazon and Atlantic forests of South America. Black with a white throat, red crissum, and a mostly dark bill with a yellow base. The bill has a distinctive groove or 'channel' along the upper edge.

children.name: Ramphastos brevis
children.description: A toucan of the Choco region in Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Ramphastos ambiguus
children.description: A large toucan with black mandible, found from Honduras to Peru.

children.name: Ramphastos aurantiirostris
children.description: A toucan with orange bill, found in the Guiana Shield.

children.name: Ramphastos culminatus
children.description: A toucan with yellow ridge on the bill, found in the western Amazon.

children.name: Ramphastos brevis
children.description: A toucan from the Choco region.

children.name: Ramphastos ambiguus
children.description: A large toucan from Honduras to Peru.

children.name: Ramphastos aurantiirostris
children.description: A toucan from the Guiana Shield.

children.name: Ramphastos culminatus
children.description: A toucan from the western Amazon.

children.name: Aulacorhynchus
children.description: Green toucanets — small, green toucans found in montane forests from Mexico to Bolivia. Their bills are patterned in black, white, yellow, and chestnut. They travel in small flocks through the canopy, eating fruit and occasionally insects.

children.name: Aulacorhynchus prasinus
children.description: A small, green toucan found in montane cloud forests from Mexico to Panama. Bright green body, pale yellow throat, and a bill patterned in black, yellow, and chestnut. Often seen in small flocks feeding on fruit at mid-elevations.

children.name: Aulacorhynchus albivittis
children.description: Found in the Andes from Venezuela to Bolivia. Green body with a distinctive white throat and a red patch at the base of the bill. Inhabits subtropical and temperate cloud forests.

children.name: Aulacorhynchus derbianus
children.description: A toucanet with chestnut tip to the bill, found in the Andes.

children.name: Aulacorhynchus whitelianus
children.description: A toucanet found in the tepui region of South America.

children.name: Aulacorhynchus haematopygus
children.description: A toucanet with crimson rump, found in the Andes.

children.name: Aulacorhynchus sulcatus
children.description: A toucanet with grooved bill, found in northern Venezuela.

children.name: Aulacorhynchus calorynchus
children.description: A toucanet with yellow eyebrow, found in the Andes.

children.name: Aulacorhynchus derbianus
children.description: A toucanet from the Andes.

children.name: Aulacorhynchus haematopygus
children.description: A toucanet from the Andes.

children.name: Pteroglossus
children.description: Aracaris — medium-sized toucans with striking plumage patterns of black, yellow, red, and green. They have serrated bills resembling a saw. More social than larger toucans, often seen in noisy flocks of up to a dozen birds. Found in lowland forests from Mexico to Argentina.

children.name: Pteroglossus aracari
children.description: Found in the Amazon Basin and the Guianas. Bright yellow belly with a red band across the chest, black head and throat, and a distinctive red-and-black patterned bill.

children.name: Pteroglossus azara
children.description: Found in western Amazonia from Colombia to Bolivia. Similar to the Black-necked Aracari but with an ivory-coloured upper mandible. Inhabits lowland rainforest and gallery forest.

children.name: Pteroglossus bitorquatus
children.description: An aracari with red collar, found in the Amazon basin.

children.name: Pteroglossus castanotis
children.description: An aracari with chestnut ear patch, found across much of South America.

children.name: Pteroglossus inscriptus
children.description: An aracari with distinctive bill markings, found in the Amazon.

children.name: Pteroglossus mariae
children.description: An aracari found in the western Amazon basin.

children.name: Pteroglossus pluricinctus
children.description: An aracari with multiple bands, found in the Amazon.

children.name: Pteroglossus sanguineus
children.description: An aracari found in the Choco region.

children.name: Pteroglossus torquatus
children.description: An aracari with red collar, found from Mexico to Colombia.

children.name: Pteroglossus viridis
children.description: A green aracari found in the Guiana Shield and northern Amazon.

children.name: Pteroglossus bitorquatus
children.description: An aracari from the Amazon.

children.name: Pteroglossus pluricinctus
children.description: An aracari with many bands from the Amazon.

children.name: Pteroglossus torquatus
children.description: An aracari from Mexico to Colombia.

children.name: Selenidera
children.description: Small toucanets with strong sexual dimorphism, found in lowland forests of South America.

children.name: Selenidera maculirostris
children.description: A toucanet with spotted bill, found in the Atlantic Forest of Brazil.

children.name: Selenidera gouldii
children.description: A toucanet found in the Amazon basin.

children.name: Selenidera reinwardtii
children.description: A toucanet with golden collar, found in the western Amazon.

children.name: Selenidera nattereri
children.description: A toucanet found in the central and eastern Amazon.

children.name: Selenidera spectabilis
children.description: A toucanet with yellow ear patch, found from Honduras to Ecuador.

children.name: Andigena
children.description: Colourful toucans of the high Andes, with bright bills and striking plumage patterns.

children.name: Andigena laminirostris
children.description: A mountain toucan with plate-like bill, found in Colombia and Ecuador.

children.name: Andigena hypoglauca
children.description: A mountain toucan with grey breast, found in the Andes from Colombia to Peru.

children.name: Andigena nigrirostris
children.description: A mountain toucan with black bill, found in the Andes.

children.name: Andigena cucullata
children.description: A mountain toucan with blue hood, found in the Andes of Peru and Bolivia.
